slides.title: Just *hang there*. Your spine will thank you.
slides.sub: A passive hang from a bar decompresses the spine and trains the grip. Here is why that matters more than it looks.

slides.title: Hang, don't pull
slides.body: A dead hang is simply letting your body weight *pull you long*. No reps, no strain, just decompression and a steady grip.

slides.title: Space for the spine
slides.body: Hanging gently lengthens the spine and opens the shoulders. Many people find it *can ease stiffness* from a day spent sitting.

slides.title: Grip is a vital sign
slides.body: Grip strength is one of the simplest markers we have. Weaker grip *is linked to* higher all-cause mortality across large studies.

slides.title: Train it by holding
slides.body: Every hang quietly builds the forearms, hands and shoulders. Strong hands *can support* an active, independent later life.

slides.title: Seconds, then minutes
slides.body: Begin with a few short holds a day. Add a little time each week and a *minute total* becomes an easy daily habit.

slides.title: Small habits, *tracked daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your movement, sleep, food and steps into one daily Health Score, so simple habits like this actually stick.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The simplest longevity move might be the one you walk past at the gym: just hang from the bar. 🌿

Hanging can decompress the spine and build grip strength, and grip is one of the strongest simple predictors of how well we age. Start with seconds and build from there. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small daily habits into one clear score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why You Should Hang From a Bar Every Day
